moduleDescription:
This module allows high-level and efficient I/O multiplexing.

Supported OS primitives: epoll, kqueue, poll and Windows select.

To use threadsafe version of this module, it needs to be compiled with both -d:threadsafe and --threads:on options.

Supported features: files, sockets, pipes, timers, processes, signals and user events.

Fully supported OS: MacOSX, FreeBSD, OpenBSD, NetBSD, Linux (except for Android).

Partially supported OS: Windows (only sockets and user events), Solaris (files, sockets, handles and user events). Android (files, sockets, handles and user events).

By default, the implementation is chosen based on the target platform; you can pass -d:nimIoselector=value to override it. Accepted values are "epoll", "kqueue", "poll", and "select".

TODO: /dev/poll, event ports and filesystem events.

entries.name: ioselSupportedPlatform
entries.code: ioselSupportedPlatform = true
entries.description: This constant is used to determine whether the destination platform is fully supported by ioselectors module.

entries.name: Selector
entries.code: Selector[T] = ref object
entries.description: An object which holds descriptors to be checked for read/write status

entries.name: IOSelectorsException
entries.code: IOSelectorsException = object of CatchableError
entries.description: Exception that is raised if an IOSelectors error occurs.

entries.name: Event
entries.code:
Event {.pure.} = enum
  Read,                     ## Descriptor is available for read
  Write,                    ## Descriptor is available for write
  Timer,                    ## Timer descriptor is completed
  Signal,                   ## Signal is raised
  Process,                  ## Process is finished
  Vnode,                    ## BSD specific file change
  User,                     ## User event is raised
  Error,                    ## Error occurred while waiting for descriptor
  VnodeWrite,               ## NOTE_WRITE (BSD specific, write to file occurred)
  VnodeDelete,              ## NOTE_DELETE (BSD specific, unlink of file occurred)
  VnodeExtend,              ## NOTE_EXTEND (BSD specific, file extended)
  VnodeAttrib,              ## NOTE_ATTRIB (BSD specific, file attributes changed)
  VnodeLink,                ## NOTE_LINK (BSD specific, file link count changed)
  VnodeRename,              ## NOTE_RENAME (BSD specific, file renamed)
  VnodeRevoke                ## NOTE_REVOKE (BSD specific, file revoke occurred)
entries.description: An enum which hold event types

entries.name: ReadyKey
entries.code:
ReadyKey = object
  fd*: int                   ## file/socket descriptor
  events*: set[Event]        ## set of events
  errorCode*: OSErrorCode    ## additional error code information for
                             ## Error events
entries.description: An object which holds result for descriptor

entries.name: SelectEvent
entries.code: SelectEvent = object
entries.description: An object which holds user defined event

entries.name: newSelector
entries.code: proc newSelector[T](): Selector[T]
entries.description: Creates a new selector

entries.name: close
entries.code: proc close[T](s: Selector[T])
entries.description: Closes the selector.

entries.name: registerHandle
entries.code:
proc registerHandle[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: int | SocketHandle;
                       events: set[Event]; data: T)
entries.description: Registers file/socket descriptor fd to selector s with events set in events. The data is application-defined data, which will be passed when an event is triggered.

entries.name: updateHandle
entries.code: proc updateHandle[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: int | SocketHandle; events: set[Event])
entries.description: Update file/socket descriptor fd, registered in selector s with new events set event.

entries.name: registerTimer
entries.code:
proc registerTimer[T](s: Selector[T]; timeout: int; oneshot: bool; data: T): int {.
    discardable.}
entries.description:
Registers timer notification with timeout (in milliseconds) to selector s.

If oneshot is true, timer will be notified only once.

Set oneshot to false if you want periodic notifications.

The data is application-defined data, which will be passed, when the timer is triggered.

Returns the file descriptor for the registered timer.

entries.name: registerSignal
entries.code: proc registerSignal[T](s: Selector[T]; signal: int; data: T): int {.discardable.}
entries.description:
Registers Unix signal notification with signal to selector s.

The data is application-defined data, which will be passed when signal raises.

Returns the file descriptor for the registered signal.

Note: This function is not supported on Windows.

entries.name: registerProcess
entries.code: proc registerProcess[T](s: Selector[T]; pid: int; data: T): int {.discardable.}
entries.description:
Registers a process id (pid) notification (when process has exited) in selector s.

The data is application-defined data, which will be passed when process with pid has exited.

Returns the file descriptor for the registered signal.

entries.name: registerEvent
entries.code: proc registerEvent[T](s: Selector[T]; ev: SelectEvent; data: T)
entries.description:
Registers selector event ev in selector s.

The data is application-defined data, which will be passed when ev happens.

entries.name: registerVnode
entries.code: proc registerVnode[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: cint; events: set[Event]; data: T)
entries.description:
Registers selector BSD/MacOSX specific vnode events for file descriptor fd and events events. data application-defined data, which to be passed, when vnode event happens.

Note: This function is supported only by BSD and MacOSX.

entries.name: newSelectEvent
entries.code: proc newSelectEvent(): SelectEvent {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a new user-defined event.

entries.name: trigger
entries.code: proc trigger(ev: SelectEvent) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Trigger event ev.

entries.name: close
entries.code: proc close(ev: SelectEvent) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Closes user-defined event ev.

entries.name: unregister
entries.code: proc unregister[T](s: Selector[T]; ev: SelectEvent)
entries.description: Unregisters user-defined event ev from selector s.

entries.name: unregister
entries.code: proc unregister[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: int | SocketHandle | cint)
entries.description: Unregisters file/socket descriptor fd from selector s.

entries.name: selectInto
entries.code:
proc selectInto[T](s: Selector[T]; timeout: int;
                   results: var openArray[ReadyKey]): int
entries.description:
Waits for events registered in selector s.

The timeout argument specifies the maximum number of milliseconds the function will be blocked for if no events are ready. Specifying a timeout of -1 causes the function to block indefinitely. All available events will be stored in results array.

Returns number of triggered events.

entries.name: select
entries.code: proc select[T](s: Selector[T]; timeout: int): seq[ReadyKey]
entries.description:
Waits for events registered in selector s.

The timeout argument specifies the maximum number of milliseconds the function will be blocked for if no events are ready. Specifying a timeout of -1 causes the function to block indefinitely.

Returns a list of triggered events.

entries.name: getData
entries.code: proc getData[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: SocketHandle | int): var T
entries.description: Retrieves application-defined data associated with descriptor fd. If specified descriptor fd is not registered, empty/default value will be returned.

entries.name: setData
entries.code: proc setData[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: SocketHandle | int; data: var T): bool
entries.description:
Associate application-defined data with descriptor fd.

Returns true, if data was successfully updated, false otherwise.

entries.name: isEmpty
entries.code: template isEmpty[T](s: Selector[T]): bool
entries.description: Returns true, if there are no registered events or descriptors in selector.

entries.name: withData
entries.code:
template withData[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: SocketHandle | int;
                     value, body: untyped)
entries.description:
Retrieves the application-data assigned with descriptor fd to value. This value can be modified in the scope of the withData call.

s.withData(fd, value) do:
  # block is executed only if `fd` registered in selector `s`
  value.uid = 1000

entries.name: withData
entries.code:
template withData[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: SocketHandle | int;
                     value, body1, body2: untyped)
entries.description:
Retrieves the application-data assigned with descriptor fd to value. This value can be modified in the scope of the withData call.

s.withData(fd, value) do:
  # block is executed only if `fd` registered in selector `s`.
  value.uid = 1000
do:
  # block is executed if `fd` not registered in selector `s`.
  raise

entries.name: contains
entries.code: proc contains[T](s: Selector[T]; fd: SocketHandle | int): bool {.inline.}
entries.description: Determines whether selector contains a file descriptor.

entries.name: getFd
entries.code: proc getFd[T](s: Selector[T]): int
entries.description:
Retrieves the underlying selector's file descriptor.

For poll and select selectors -1 is returned.
